easter-bunny-basket-cake.name: Easter Bunny Basket Cake
instructions: Make the chocolate eggs: In a medium microwave-safe bowl, add the dark blue candy melts and 2 teaspoons coconut oil. Melt in the microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring between, until smooth.
Use a fork to drizzle flecks of the melted dark blue chocolate into the cavity of 4 egg-shaped chocolate molds. Place in the freezer for 10-15 minutes, until firm.

Meanwhile, in another medium microwave-safe bowl, add the light blue candy melts and the remaining 1 teaspoon coconut oil. Melt in the microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring between, until smooth.

Spoon a dollop of the melted light blue chocolate into each cavity of the chocolate molds, then spread in an even layer all around the molds, leaving the centers hollow. Place in the freezer for about 30 minutes, or until solid.

Remove 2 cylinders at a time from the molds. Fill 1 cylinder with some of the Funfetti® sprinkles (from the lid of the frosting tub). Dip the edges of the other cylinder lightly in the melted light blue chocolate, then place on top of the sprinkle-filled cylinder. Place on a baking sheet and repeat with the remaining 2 cylinders. Place the chocolate eggs in the freezer for 2 minutes, or until set.

Make the magical cherry: Fill a medium bowl with cold water and add the gelatin sheets. Let sit for 5 minutes, then wring out excess water and place in a large bowl. Add the boiling water and Jell-O mix and whisk together until gelatin is dissolved. Pour the mixture into a silicone cherry-shaped mold and refrigerate for 4-6 hours, until firm.
Unmold the Jell-O cherry and brush with the edible glitter. Cut the Tootsie roll into quarters and shape into stems. Place the stems on top of the Jell-O cherry and set aside.

Make the fruit streamers: Wrap each fruit strip around a thick straw, cover with plastic wrap, and let sit for 30-60 minutes, until they hold their shape. Carefully remove the fruit spirals from the straws and set aside.
Make the cake: Preheat the oven to 177°C (180°C). Grease 4 (8-inch) round cake pans with nonstick spray.

In a large bowl, beat together the Pillsbury™ Funfetti® cake mix, water, eggs, and oil with an electric mixer on medium speed for 2 minutes. Pour the batter into the prepared cake pans. Bake for 34-38 minutes, until a toothpick inserted into the centers of the cakes comes out clean. Let sit for 10-15 minutes before removing to a wire rack to cool completely.

Assemble the cake: Using a large serrated knife, slice a thin layer off the top of each cake layer to create a flat surface; discard the scraps. Smear a bit of the Pillsbury™ Funfetti® frosting onto an 8-inch cardboard cake round set on top of a cake stand, then place a cake layer on the round. Use a large offset spatula to spread an even layer of frosting over the cake. Place another cake layer on top and frost. Repeat with the remaining 2 cake layers, placing the last cake layer on top, bottom-side up. Spread a thin layer of frosting over the top and sides of the cake to make the crumb coat. Refrigerate for about 30 minutes, or until the frosting is set.

Frost the cake: Fit a pastry bag with a basket weave tip and fill with frosting. Pipe a basket weave design on the sides of the cake, leaving the top untouched. Refrigerate for about 30 minutes, or until the frosting is set.
Decorate the cake: Add edible grass to the top of the cake. Decorate with the Jell-O cherry, 2 chocolate eggs, jelly beans, fruit streamers, and the remaining Funfetti® sprinkles.
Thread the marshmallow rope onto skewers and shape into a handle. Place on top of the cake, and enjoy!

easter-turkey-leg-teleculinaria.name: Easter Turkey Leg
instructions: Prepare the marinade: place all the ingredients indicated in a blender, with the exception of white wine, and crush until you get a paste.

Peel the onions, cut them in circles and dispose of them at the bottom of a tray. Put the turkey leg on top, bar it with the previous marinade, water it with white wine, tape it with aluminium foil and take it to the oven, preheated at 160°C, for 1 hour and 30 minutes.

After the specified time, remove the tray from the oven, add the potatoes, previously peeled and cut into cubes, and the purple onions, cut into thick half-moons. Take it back to the oven, preheated at 170°C, without the foil, for about 45 minutes. When it's all good and golden, remove it from the oven and serve.

eggplant-curry-teleculinaria.name: Eggplant Curry
instructions: Misture a garam masala, o gengibre e a canela e reserve. Lave e corte a beringela a gosto. Descasque e pique a cebola e os alhos.
Melt half the butter in a frying pan, add the onion and let it caramelize until it's golden. Then add garlic, tomato paste and spice mixture, stir and let cook for about 2 minutes.
Add the tomato cubes, season with salt and pepper and let it boil. Then let it cook for 10 minutes. Then add the creams, wrap it well, crush everything with the magic wand and remove from the fire.
Light a frying pan with the remaining butter and let it melt. Add the eggplant, season with salt and let it cook until it is soft but not undone.

However, prepare the rice: take to the fire a pot with a thread of olive oil and the onion, previously peeled and chopped and let saute, for 2 minutes. Then add the rice, water with the water and let it cook for 10 to 12 minutes. Remove and dispose in a bowl.
Put the curry sauce in a bowl and add the eggplant. Sprinkle with chopped chives and serve with cooked rice, also sprinkled with chopped chives.

elote-dip.name: Elote Dip
instructions: Combine Mexican crema, lime juice, mayonnaise, cayenne pepper, salt, and cumin in a large bowl until well blended.
Add corn, cotija cheese, cilantro, and serrano pepper; stir until well combined.

enjoy-this-delicious-tuna-and-hard-boile.name: Enjoy this Delicious Tuna And Hard-boiled Egg Wrap
enjoy-this-delicious-tuna-and-hard-boile.original_name: Disfruta de este rico wrap de atún y huevo duro
instructions: In a pot with enough water, cook your eggs till they are cooked.
While the eggs are made, open the tuna in a can, snap and add into a medium bowl.
Season with salt and pepper to taste and add the two tablespoons of mayonnaise. It's a good mix till it's creamy.
Wash the lettuce leaves and cut into a tomato.
Once the minutes of egg cooking have passed, remove from the water and let rest.
Remove the skin from the eggs and cut into slices.
Warm the wrap, extending the temperature flour omelette, place a lettuce leaf and add a portion of tuna.
It incorporates baked egg slices to taste and cuts of tomato. Wrap up the wrap, hold up very well and ready, you'll have a taste.
Enjoy!

enjoy-this-garlic-grouper-comedera.name: Enjoy This Garlic Grouper
enjoy-this-garlic-grouper-comedera.original_name: Disfruta de este mero al ajillo
instructions: Put up a frying pan with very little oil.
Sazona con sal y perejil los filetes.
When the oil's hot, add the garlic previously peeled and steamed.
When the garlic are golden, add the steaks, add the wine let them evaporate for about 5 minutes.
Rectifica la sal y agrega la mantequilla.
Serve with your preferred accompaniment and enjoy.

enjoy-this-roast-beef-picana-comedera.name: Enjoy That Roast Beef Pig
enjoy-this-roast-beef-picana-comedera.original_name: Disfruta de esta picaña de ternera asada
instructions: Vamos a iniciar nuestra receta, sazonando las piezas de carne.
Take your picket cuts and put them with thick salt and pepper and add some extra virgin olive oil to them. Marina for 15 minutes.
Pasado ese tiempo, engrasa las rejillas de tu parrilla o grill.
Put the pits to the grill and cook them for 6 minutes on each side. What we're after is that the flesh looks tender and juicy. I mean, get to the point!
If you like them better, leave them for longer.
That's it! We have a classic and delicious and juicy meat.

enzos-spaghetti-allamatriciana.name: Enzo's Spaghetti All'amatriciana
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add guanciale and cook, stirring frequently until slightly browned, about 7 minutes. Remove guanciale to a plate. Leave a bit of the rendered fat in the skillet and discard the rest.

Add onion to the skillet and cook and stir until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and red pepper flakes; cook for 1 minute. Return guanciale to the skillet and cook for 1 to 2 minutes. Pour in white wine and cook until wine has reduced slightly, about 3 minutes.

Stir in canned tomatoes, breaking up whole tomatoes with a spoon. Pour 1/3 cup water into the tomato can, swirl around to rinse the can, and pour into the skillet with remaining 2/3 cup water and tomato paste. Bring sauce to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour.

Meanwhile, bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, 10 to 12 minutes. Ladle out about 1/2 cup of the pasta water, add to the sauce in the skillet, and mix well. Drain spaghetti and stir into the sauce until well combined.
Sprinkle generous amounts of Pecorino Romano cheese over each serving, and garnish with basil.

esers-balsamic-salad-dressing.name: Eser's Balsamic Salad Dressing

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Combine balsamic vinegar, honey, mustard, garlic, oregano, salt, and black pepper in a food processor. Turn the food processor on; pour olive oil in a steady stream into balsamic vinegar mixture until salad dressing is thick and creamy.

espresso-fudge-pops.name: Espresso Fudge Pops

ingredients.item: . instant espresso powder or unsweetened cocoa powder

instructions: Whisk 1 cup (200 g) granulated sugar, ¾ cup (63 g) unsweetened cocoa powder, 2 Tbsp. instant espresso powder or unsweetened cocoa powder, 2 Tbsp. cornstarch or potato starch, and ¼ tsp. kosher salt in a medium saucepan. Add 1 cup whole milk, whisking until a smooth paste forms, then whisk in remaining 2½ cups milk. Place saucepan over medium heat; bring to a simmer. Cook, whisking often, until very hot to the touch. Switch to a heatproof rubber spatula; continue to cook, stirring constantly and scraping down sides of pan, until thickened, 3–4 minutes more. Remove from heat and whisk in 1 tsp. vanilla extract. Let cool 10 minutes, whisking occasionally to prevent a skin from forming.
Pour mixture into molds; freeze until just starting to set, about 1 hour. Insert sticks; freeze until solid, about 5 hours.

To serve, run ice pops under hot water for several seconds and unmold.
Do ahead: Ice pops can be made 3 months ahead. Cover molds tightly if needed and keep frozen.

escalivada-recipe-comedera.name: Retirement

ingredients.item: Olive oil (or otherwise other vegetable oil)

instructions: We'll start by pressurizing the oven at 160 degrees.
Then we will use the time that the oven takes to warm up, to wash and prepare the vegetables. The first will be eggplant. We'll skin her and cut her into something tops.
After cutting the eggplant, we will prepare the oven plate for the cooking of the plants. I'd rather cover her with paper. Nevertheless, there are cookies that simply gain her.

Then we'll put the eggplant strips into a bowl and water them with an oil thread and pat them to taste. We will mix everything well, so that the pieces of eggplant will be evenly filled and sewed and put them onto the oven plate.
We will place the eggplant tapes in a single layer onto the plate, so that they are cooked evenly. We'll put them in the oven (right in the middle) and get back to care about the other plants.
Now we'll wash the peppers. We'll clear our heads and open them and remove their seeds and their inner white parts. Then we'll cut his skin into strips.
In the case of onion, we'll peel and then we'll mince it in Jolian.
Just as we did with the eggplant earlier, we will place the ion and the peppers in a bowl, we will have them with an oil threat and we will pat them to taste. Then we'll mix everything up.
To continue, we will open the oven and place the pits and ions on the plate, and leave them with eggplants.
Now we'll come and get the pumpkins. We'll wash them well and cut them into strips. We'll pat and get them into the oven with the other vegetables.
From this point of view we have to monitor plants often. It's vital that they don't get toasted. We'll let them cook about 30 minutes. While this happens, we'll peel our garlic teeth.
We'll stick every foot into as few pieces as we can and put them back.

When the cooking time of the tidal phase is up, we will get the vegetables out of the oven and we will also get the juice back. We'll put everything into a bowl and spring with the garlic we've been pounding before.
We will also have an olive oil threat distributed about the production.
Test the plate to check and if necessary fix with more salt and / or pepper.
